paper.title: A single-cell transcriptomic atlas characterizes ageing tissues in the mouse

glossary.Tabula Muris Senis: The Mouse Ageing Cell Atlas; a single-cell transcriptomic atlas across the lifespan of Mus musculus covering 23 tissues and organs at six age groups
glossary.Tabula Muris: The previously published single-cell transcriptomic atlas of 20 mouse organs at 3 months of age, used as a reference for cell-type annotation
glossary.FACS: Fluorescence-activated cell sorting; here used to sort single cells into microtitre well plates for full-length transcript sequencing via Smart-seq2
glossary.droplet: Microfluidic droplet-based single-cell RNA sequencing using the 10x Genomics Chromium platform for 3-prime end counting
glossary.Cdkn2a: Gene encoding the p16 tumor suppressor protein; a widely used marker of cellular senescence
glossary.Sirt: Sirtuin gene family; NAD-dependent deacetylases implicated in delaying cellular senescence
glossary.Umod: Gene encoding uromodulin, the most abundant protein in urine, produced by epithelial cells lining the thick ascending limb of the loop of Henle
glossary.Mrc1: Mannose receptor C-type 1 gene; encodes the mannose receptor involved in immune defense
glossary.LSECs: Liver sinusoidal endothelial cells; specialized endothelial cells with a unique role in hepatic immune defense
glossary.ERCC: External RNA Controls Consortium spike-in RNA standards used to estimate technical error rates in sequencing
glossary.MHC: Major histocompatibility complex; a set of cell surface proteins essential for adaptive immune system recognition
glossary.AP1: Activator protein 1 transcription factor complex, including Jun and Fos family members
glossary.Jchain: Gene encoding the joining chain of immunoglobulins, also known as Igj; a marker of plasma cells
glossary.bbknn: Batch balanced k-nearest neighbours; a batch-alignment algorithm used to correct for method-associated batch effects
glossary.MAST: Model-based Analysis of Single-cell Transcriptomics; a statistical framework for differential expression analysis treating age as a continuous covariate
glossary.TraCeR: Computational tool for reconstructing T cell receptor sequences from single-cell RNA-seq data
glossary.singlecell-ige: Computational tool for reconstructing B cell receptor sequences from single-cell RNA-seq data
glossary.PAGA: Partition-based graph abstraction; a method for reconstructing developmental or ageing trajectories from single-cell data
glossary.diversity score: Shannon entropy of cluster assignments for a given cell type, regressed against age to detect systematic changes in transcriptomic heterogeneity
glossary.clonotype: A group of B cells or T cells sharing the same receptor sequence, indicating clonal expansion from a common ancestor

nodes.claim_text: Single-cell transcriptomic profiling across the mouse lifespan reveals cell-type-specific molecular changes and tissue compositional shifts that characterize ageing across multiple organs.
nodes.evidence_location: Abstract; Title; Discussion (page 594)
nodes.supporting_quote: Using single-cell transcriptomic data, we assessed cell-type-specific manifestations of different hallmarks of ageing-such as senescence, genomic instability and changes in the immune system.

nodes.claim_text: The Tabula Muris Senis provides a comprehensive single-cell transcriptomic resource that reveals how the most important hallmarks of ageing are reflected across a broad range of tissues and cell types.
nodes.evidence_location: Abstract; Discussion (page 594)
nodes.supporting_quote: This transcriptomic atlas-which we denote Tabula Muris Senis, or 'Mouse Ageing Cell Atlas'-provides molecular information about how the most important hallmarks of ageing are reflected in a broad range of tissues and cell types.

nodes.claim_text: The Tabula Muris Senis is a validated, manually curated single-cell transcriptomic atlas spanning 23 tissues across six age groups, enabling cell-type-specific analyses of ageing.
nodes.experiment_type: single-cell RNA-seq (FACS Smart-seq2 and 10x Genomics droplet)
nodes.experiment_detail: Single-cell RNA sequencing was performed on more than 350,000 cells from male and female C57BL/6JN mice at six age groups (1, 3, 18, 21, 24, and 30 months) across 23 tissues using both FACS-based Smart-seq2 and 10x Genomics Chromium droplet platforms. After quality filtering, 110,824 FACS cells and 245,389 droplet cells were retained.
nodes.analysis_type: cell type annotation and cross-method validation
nodes.analysis_detail: Cells were clustered per tissue across all ages using PCA, Louvain and Leiden graph-based clustering. The Tabula Muris 3-month data was used as reference for semi-automated annotation, propagating labels for clusters with 1:1 mapping. Remaining clusters were manually annotated by tissue experts. Cross-method batch correction was performed using bbknn, followed by whole-organism Louvain clustering to assess annotation consistency.
nodes.evidence_location: Figure 1; Main text paragraphs 1-2 (page 590)
nodes.supporting_quote: We performed single-cell RNA sequencing on more than 350,000 cells from male and female C57BL/6JN mice belonging to six age groups, ranging from 1 month (the equivalent of human early childhood) to 30 months (the equivalent of a human centenarian).

nodes.claim_text: Cellular senescence markers, particularly Cdkn2a, show age-related increases in both the fraction of expressing cells and per-cell expression levels across the organism.
nodes.evidence_location: Figure 2a-d; Main text paragraph 3 (page 590-591); Supplementary Table 4
nodes.supporting_quote: The expression of Cdkn2a and its protein product p16 is one of the most frequently used markers of senescence and is an important hallmark of ageing.

nodes.claim_text: The cellular composition of tissues changes with age, with distinct cell-type proportional shifts observed in the bladder, kidney, spleen, mammary gland, liver, bone marrow, and skin.
nodes.evidence_location: Figures 2e-j; Extended Data Figures 5-6; Main text (pages 591-593)
nodes.supporting_quote: The cellular composition of each tissue type tends to vary with age.

nodes.claim_text: Genomic instability, as measured by somatic mutation accumulation in transcribed genes, increases with age across all analysed organs and cell types.
nodes.evidence_location: Figure 3; Extended Data Figures 7-8; Main text (page 593)
nodes.supporting_quote: Genomic instability is among the most widely studied hallmarks of ageing, and full-length transcript data enables analysis of the accumulation of somatic mutations with age.

nodes.claim_text: The adaptive immune system shows age-related changes including increased clonality of both B cell and T cell repertoires, suggesting reduced capacity to respond to new pathogens.
nodes.evidence_location: Figure 4a-b; Extended Data Figure 9; Main text (pages 593-594)
nodes.supporting_quote: These changes in clonality for both B cell and T cell repertoires are noteworthy, because they suggest that the immune system of a 24-month-old mouse is less likely to respond to new pathogens.

nodes.claim_text: Immune cell populations in the brain and kidney undergo significant age-related transcriptomic diversification, with microglia acquiring a pro-inflammatory and neurodegeneration-associated signature and kidney macrophages shifting from an anti-inflammatory to a pro-inflammatory state.
nodes.evidence_location: Figure 4c-f; Extended Data Figures 10-11; Main text (pages 593-594)
nodes.supporting_quote: We observed significant changes in diversity for cells of the immune system that originate from the brain and the kidney (Fig. 4c, Extended Data Fig. 11a, b).

nodes.claim_text: Single-cell data resolves that bulk gene expression changes with age are often attributable to both changes in cell-type proportions and changes in per-cell gene expression levels.
nodes.evidence_location: Extended Data Figure 5a-b; Main text paragraph 3 (page 590)
nodes.supporting_quote: we observed that-in many cases-changes in gene expression are due to both changes in the numbers of cells in a population and changes in the gene expression levels in each cell

nodes.claim_text: The bladder undergoes pronounced age-related changes in cellular composition and gene expression, with a shift from mesenchymal to urothelial dominance, declining vascularization, and increasing inflammatory signatures.
nodes.evidence_location: Figure 2e-f; Main text (page 591); Supplementary Table 6
nodes.supporting_quote: The bladder shows pronounced changes in cell-type composition with age (Fig. 2e)

nodes.claim_text: Age-related changes in the kidney include declining glomerular and tubular cell populations with compensatory increases in local gene expression, suggesting impaired kidney function.
nodes.evidence_location: Figure 2g-h; Main text (pages 591-592); Supplementary Table 6
nodes.supporting_quote: Both mesangial cells and capillary endothelial cells are core glomerular cells, and the reduction in their relative abundances with age (Fig. 2g, top)-together with a tissue-wide reduction of Egf and Atp1a1 expression (Fig. 2h, Supplementary Table 6)-suggest an impaired glomerular filtration rate

nodes.claim_text: The spleen shows age-related shifts in immune cell composition, with declining T cells and increasing plasma cells.
nodes.evidence_location: Figure 2i-j; Main text (page 592); Supplementary Table 5-6
nodes.supporting_quote: In the spleen, the proportion of T cells decreases with age while the relative amount of plasma cells increases (Fig. 2i).

nodes.claim_text: The liver undergoes age-related changes including declining hepatocyte proportions, increased immune gene signatures, and altered Kupffer cell and sinusoidal endothelial cell phenotypes.
nodes.evidence_location: Extended Data Figure 6; Main text (page 593); Supplementary Tables 6-7
nodes.supporting_quote: The tissue composition of the liver also changes with age.

nodes.claim_text: The mammary gland shows age-related T cell decline and upregulation of AP1 transcription factor genes.
nodes.evidence_location: Extended Data Figure 5c-d; Main text (pages 592-593); Supplementary Table 6
nodes.supporting_quote: Similarly, in the mammary gland we observed a decline of the T cell population (Extended Data Fig. 5c).

nodes.claim_text: B cell clonality increases progressively with age across the organism.
nodes.evidence_location: Figure 4a; Main text (pages 593-594); Supplementary Table 9
nodes.supporting_quote: whereas most of the cells at 3 months were not part of a clone (9% were part of a clonal family), the number of B cells belonging to a clonotype doubled at 18 months (20%) when compared to 3 months, and doubled again from 18 months to 24 months (around 38%).

nodes.claim_text: T cell clonality increases markedly with age across the organism.
nodes.evidence_location: Figure 4b; Main text (pages 593-594); Supplementary Table 9
nodes.supporting_quote: only around 3% (55 out of 1,895) of the cells at 3 months were part of a clone. At 18 months and 24 months, this value increased to around 23% (479 out of 2,056) and around 20% (348 out of 1,780) of the cells, respectively

nodes.claim_text: Brain microglia undergo age-related transcriptomic changes characterized by expansion of a pro-inflammatory subset with interferon-responsive and neurodegeneration-associated gene signatures.
nodes.evidence_location: Figure 4c-e; Extended Data Figure 11a,c,e; Main text (page 594); Supplementary Table 10
nodes.supporting_quote: gene expression in clusters 10, 12 and 14 is enriched with interferon responsive or regulatory genes (for example, Oasl2, Oas1a, Ifit3, Rtp4, Bst2, Stat1, Irf7, Ifitm3, Usp18, Ifi204 and Ifit2), suggesting an expansion of this small pro-inflammatory subset of microglia in the ageing brain

nodes.claim_text: Kidney macrophages shift from an anti-inflammatory (M2) to a pro-inflammatory (M1) transcriptomic state with age.
nodes.evidence_location: Figure 4f; Extended Data Figure 11b,d,f; Main text (page 594); Supplementary Table 10
nodes.supporting_quote: Differential gene expression revealed that cluster 10 is enriched for an M2-macrophage gene signature (for example, Il10, H2-Eb1, H2-Ab1, H2-Aa, Cd74, C1qa, Cxcl16, Hexb, Cd81, C1qb and Cd72) whereas cluster 13 resembles a M1-proinflammatory macrophage state (for example, Hp, Itgal, Msrb1 and Gngt2)

nodes.claim_text: Cdkn2a expression increases with age in both the fraction of expressing cells and per-cell expression levels, with the strongest age-correlation among senescence markers.
nodes.evidence_location: Figure 2a-d; Main text paragraph 3 (page 590-591); Supplementary Table 4
nodes.supporting_quote: Cdkn2a has the highest correlation between ageing and the fraction of cells in which it is expressed

nodes.claim_text: The fraction of cells expressing sirtuin family genes decreases with age, consistent with their role in delaying cellular senescence.
nodes.evidence_location: Main text paragraph 3 (page 591); Supplementary Table 4
nodes.supporting_quote: For some genes, including members of the Sirt family (Sirt3, Sirt4 and Sirt5), the fraction of cells in which they were expressed was found to decrease with age; this is consistent with previous literature finding that sirtuins-the protein products of Sirt genes-are essential in delaying cellular senescence

nodes.claim_text: Cdkn2a and its protein product p16 is one of the most frequently used markers of senescence and is an important hallmark of ageing (Rayess et al. 2012; Hernandez-Segura et al. 2018).
nodes.evidence_location: Main text paragraph 3 (page 590-591); citing refs 10, 11
nodes.supporting_quote: The expression of Cdkn2a and its protein product p16 is one of the most frequently used markers of senescence and is an important hallmark of ageing.

nodes.claim_text: Sirtuins are essential in delaying cellular senescence (Saunders & Verdin 2007; Lee et al. 2019).
nodes.evidence_location: Main text paragraph 3 (page 591); citing refs 20, 21
nodes.supporting_quote: this is consistent with previous literature finding that sirtuins-the protein products of Sirt genes-are essential in delaying cellular senescence

nodes.claim_text: Age-related decline of T cell populations has been associated with increased risk of infectious disease and cancer (Palmer et al. 2018).
nodes.evidence_location: Main text (page 592); citing ref 33
nodes.supporting_quote: Age-related decline of T cell populations has been associated with increased risk of infectious disease and cancer

nodes.claim_text: Normal mammary gland involution is accompanied by increased expression of AP1 transcription factor family genes (Shen et al. 2006; Girnius et al. 2018).
nodes.evidence_location: Main text (pages 592-593); citing refs 34, 35
nodes.supporting_quote: consistent with the observation that normal involution of the mammary gland is accompanied by increased expression of this gene family

nodes.claim_text: Pro-inflammatory macrophages drive cellular senescence, and Il1b shows markedly different expression in the liver with age (Covarrubias et al. 2019).
nodes.evidence_location: Main text (page 593); citing ref 12
nodes.supporting_quote: Previous findings suggested that pro-inflammatory macrophages drive cellular senescence, and identified the gene Il1b as showing markedly different expression in the liver with age

nodes.claim_text: Specific blocking of IL1-RI in hepatocytes attenuates cell death after injury, supporting the idea that increased Il1b expression in Kupffer cells is a poor prognostic indicator (Gehrke et al. 2018).
nodes.evidence_location: Main text (page 593); citing ref 37
nodes.supporting_quote: Specific blocking of IL1-RI in hepatocytes has been shown to attenuate cell death after injury, supporting the idea that increased expression of Il1b in Kupffer cells is typically a poor prognostic

nodes.claim_text: Liver sinusoidal endothelial cells are the main carriers of the mannose receptor (MRC1) in the liver (Liu et al. 2013).
nodes.evidence_location: Main text (page 593); citing ref 38
nodes.supporting_quote: Liver sinusoidal endothelial cells (LSECs) have a unique role in immune defence, being the main carriers of the mannose receptor (MRC1) in this organ

nodes.claim_text: A unique microglia type associated with restricting development of Alzheimer's disease has been identified with a specific transcriptomic signature (Keren-Shaul et al. 2017).
nodes.evidence_location: Main text (page 594); citing ref 47
nodes.supporting_quote: the list of differentially expressed genes between 'young' and 'old' clusters resembles the previously reported Alzheimer's disease-specific microglial signature

nodes.claim_text: Older individuals have higher vulnerability to new infections and lower benefits from vaccination compared with younger individuals (Goronzy & Weyand 2013, 2017).
nodes.evidence_location: Main text (page 594); citing refs 45, 46
nodes.supporting_quote: This corroborates literature reports suggesting that older individuals have a higher vulnerability to new infections and lower benefits from vaccination compared with younger individuals

nodes.claim_text: Downregulation of ATP1A1 is associated with impaired function in renal cell carcinoma, and EGF serves as a prognostic biomarker in chronic kidney diseases (Zhang et al. 2017; Isaka 2016).
nodes.evidence_location: Main text (pages 591-592); citing refs 29, 30
nodes.supporting_quote: together with a tissue-wide reduction of Egf and Atp1a1 expression (Fig. 2h, Supplementary Table 6)-suggest an impaired glomerular filtration rate

nodes.claim_text: M1 and M2 macrophage states are defined by distinct marker gene sets (Jablonski et al. 2015).
nodes.evidence_location: Main text (page 594); citing ref 50
nodes.supporting_quote: cluster 13 resembles a M1-proinflammatory macrophage state

nodes.claim_text: The proportion of cells expressing Cdkn2a more than doubled in older mice compared with younger mice in both FACS and droplet datasets.
nodes.experiment_type: single-cell RNA-seq (FACS Smart-seq2 and 10x Genomics droplet)
nodes.experiment_detail: Single-cell RNA-seq was performed on cells from C57BL/6JN mice at six age groups (1, 3, 18, 21, 24, 30 months) across 23 tissues using both FACS-based Smart-seq2 and 10x Genomics droplet platforms. n = 44,518, 34,027 and 31,551 cells for FACS at 3, 18 and 24 months; n = 25,980, 45,602, 44,645, 35,828, 37,660 and 55,674 cells for droplet at 1, 3, 18, 21, 24 and 30 months.
nodes.analysis_type: fraction of cells expressing a marker gene across age groups
nodes.analysis_detail: For each age group, the fraction of all cells expressing Cdkn2a (>0 counts) was computed. Age groups were compared using Mann-Whitney-Wilcoxon rank-sum two-sided test. Analysis was performed separately for FACS and droplet datasets.
nodes.evidence_location: Figure 2a-d; Main text - paragraph 3 (page 590-591)
nodes.supporting_quote: The proportion of cells expressing Cdkn2a more than doubled in older mice compared with younger mice according to analysis by both FACS (Fig. 2a) and droplet (Fig. 2b) methods; this was accompanied by a twofold increase in the expression levels of p16 in cells in which it was expressed (Fig. 2c, d).

nodes.claim_text: The mean expression level of Cdkn2a approximately doubled in cells that express it when comparing old to young mice in both FACS and droplet data.
nodes.experiment_type: single-cell RNA-seq (FACS Smart-seq2 and 10x Genomics droplet)
nodes.experiment_detail: Single-cell RNA-seq was performed on cells from C57BL/6JN mice at six age groups across 23 tissues using both FACS and droplet platforms. Same sample sizes as L1.
nodes.analysis_type: per-cell expression level comparison across age groups
nodes.analysis_detail: For cells expressing Cdkn2a (>0 counts), the median expression level (log-transformed and scaled) was computed per age group. Age groups were compared using Mann-Whitney-Wilcoxon rank-sum two-sided test. Analysis was performed separately for FACS and droplet datasets.
nodes.evidence_location: Figure 2c-d; Main text - paragraph 3 (page 590-591)
nodes.supporting_quote: this was accompanied by a twofold increase in the expression levels of p16 in cells in which it was expressed (Fig. 2c, d).

nodes.claim_text: In 30-month-old mice the fraction of cells expressing Cdkn2a was smaller than in 24-month-old mice.
nodes.experiment_detail: Droplet-based single-cell RNA-seq on cells from C57BL/6JN mice at 24 months (n = 37,660 cells) and 30 months (n = 55,674 cells).
nodes.analysis_type: fraction of cells expressing a marker gene across age groups
nodes.analysis_detail: The fraction of all cells expressing Cdkn2a (>0 counts) was computed for 24-month and 30-month age groups in the droplet dataset and compared.
nodes.evidence_location: Figure 2b; Main text - paragraph 3 (page 590-591)
nodes.supporting_quote: Notably, in 30-month-old mice the fraction of cells that expressed p16 was smaller than in 24-month-old mice, perhaps because long-living animals have a slower rate of senescence.

nodes.claim_text: Among previously characterized senescence markers, Cdkn2a showed the highest correlation between ageing and the fraction of cells in which it is expressed.
nodes.experiment_type: single-cell RNA-seq (FACS Smart-seq2 and 10x Genomics droplet)
nodes.experiment_detail: Single-cell RNA-seq data from all age groups across all tissues. A curated list of previously characterized senescence markers was used (Supplementary Table 4).
nodes.analysis_type: correlation of senescence marker expression fraction with age
nodes.analysis_detail: For each gene in a curated list of senescence markers (Supplementary Table 4), the fraction of cells expressing the gene was computed per age group across all tissues. The correlation between age and fraction of expressing cells was computed. Cdkn2a was identified as having the highest positive correlation.
nodes.evidence_location: Main text - paragraph 3 (page 591); Supplementary Table 4
nodes.supporting_quote: Cdkn2a has the highest correlation between ageing and the fraction of cells in which it is expressed; other genes with positive correlation include E2f2, Lmnb1, Tnf and Itgax.

nodes.claim_text: The fraction of cells expressing members of the Sirt family (Sirt3, Sirt4, Sirt5) decreased with age.
nodes.experiment_type: single-cell RNA-seq (FACS Smart-seq2 and 10x Genomics droplet)
nodes.experiment_detail: Single-cell RNA-seq data from all age groups across all tissues.
nodes.analysis_type: fraction of cells expressing senescence-associated genes across age groups
nodes.analysis_detail: For Sirt3, Sirt4, and Sirt5, the fraction of cells expressing each gene was computed per age group across all tissues. A decrease with age was observed.
nodes.evidence_location: Main text - paragraph 3 (page 591); Supplementary Table 4
nodes.supporting_quote: For some genes, including members of the Sirt family (Sirt3, Sirt4 and Sirt5), the fraction of cells in which they were expressed was found to decrease with age

nodes.claim_text: In the bladder, the mesenchymal cell compartment decreased by approximately threefold over the mouse lifespan while the urothelial compartment increased by a similar amount.
nodes.experiment_detail: Droplet-based single-cell RNA-seq on bladder cells from C57BL/6JN mice at 1, 3, 18, and 24 months. n = 970, 3,804, 2,739 and 3,864 cells respectively.
nodes.analysis_type: cell type proportion analysis across age
nodes.analysis_detail: For each age group, the relative proportion of each cell type in the bladder was computed. Linear regression of relative cell type proportion against age was performed using scipy.stats linregress. Significance was assessed at P < 0.05 with r2 > 0.7 using two-sided Wald test with t-distribution.
nodes.evidence_location: Figure 2e; Main text - bladder composition (page 591)
nodes.supporting_quote: whereas the mesenchymal compartment of this tissue decreases by a factor of three over the lifetime of the mouse (Fig. 2e, left), the urothelial compartment increases by a similar amount (Fig. 2e, right).

nodes.claim_text: Differential gene expression analysis of the bladder showed downregulation of stromal-associated genes (Col1a1, Col1a2, Col3a1, Dcn) and upregulation of epithelial-associated genes (Krt15, Krt18, Sfn) with age.
nodes.experiment_detail: Droplet-based single-cell RNA-seq on bladder cells from C57BL/6JN mice at 1, 3, 18, and 24 months. n = 970, 3,804, 2,739 and 3,864 cells respectively.
nodes.analysis_type: differential gene expression analysis with age as continuous covariate
nodes.analysis_detail: Differential gene expression was performed on bladder tissue using MAST, treating age as a continuous numerical covariate while controlling for sex and technology. Genes were classified as significant under FDR threshold of 0.01 and age coefficient threshold of 0.005 (corresponding to approximately 10% fold change).
nodes.evidence_location: Figure 2f; Main text - bladder composition (page 591); Supplementary Table 6
nodes.supporting_quote: Using differential gene expression analysis to assess overall changes in tissues with age, we found that stromal-associated genes (Col1a1, Col1a2, Col3a1 and Dcn) are downregulated while epithelial-associated genes (Krt15, Krt18 and Sfn) are upregulated, supporting the compositional observations

nodes.claim_text: The bladder endothelial cell population declined with age, and vasculature-associated genes Htra1 and Fos were downregulated.
nodes.experiment_detail: Droplet-based single-cell RNA-seq on bladder cells from C57BL/6JN mice at 1, 3, 18, and 24 months.
nodes.analysis_type: cell type proportion analysis and differential gene expression
nodes.analysis_detail: Relative proportion of endothelial cells was computed per age group. Differential gene expression was performed using MAST with age as continuous covariate, controlling for sex and technology. FDR < 0.01 and age coefficient > 0.005.
nodes.evidence_location: Figure 2e-f; Main text - bladder composition (page 591); Supplementary Table 6
nodes.supporting_quote: The decline of the endothelial population suggests that bladder ageing in mice might be associated with lower organ vascularization, which is consistent with recent findings and with the observed downregulation of vasculature-associated genes Htra1 and Fos

nodes.claim_text: The bladder leukocyte population increased with age, accompanied by overexpression of Lgals3, Igfbp2, and Ly6d across the tissue.
nodes.experiment_detail: Droplet-based single-cell RNA-seq on bladder cells from C57BL/6JN mice at 1, 3, 18, and 24 months.
nodes.analysis_type: cell type proportion analysis and differential gene expression
nodes.analysis_detail: Relative proportion of leukocytes was computed per age group. Differential gene expression was performed using MAST with age as continuous covariate, controlling for sex and technology. FDR < 0.01 and age coefficient > 0.005.
nodes.evidence_location: Figure 2e-f; Main text - bladder composition (page 591); Supplementary Table 6
nodes.supporting_quote: The increase in the leukocyte population could indicate an inflammatory tissue microenvironment, a common hallmark of ageing that is consistent with literature on overactive bladders and is supported by a significant overexpression of Lgals3, Igfbp2 and Ly6d across the tissue

nodes.claim_text: Old bladder leukocytes showed increased expression of pro-inflammatory markers (Cd14, Lgals3, Tnfrsf12a) and decreased expression of anti-inflammatory markers (Cd9, Cd81) compared with young leukocytes.
nodes.experiment_detail: Droplet-based single-cell RNA-seq on bladder leukocytes from C57BL/6JN mice, comparing young and old age groups.
nodes.analysis_type: differential gene expression within a cell type across age
nodes.analysis_detail: Differential gene expression was performed on bladder leukocytes comparing old vs young age groups using MAST, treating age as continuous covariate while controlling for sex and technology. FDR < 0.01 and age coefficient > 0.005.
nodes.evidence_location: Main text - bladder composition (page 591); Supplementary Table 6
nodes.supporting_quote: we observed that old leukocytes show increased expression of pro-inflammatory markers-such as Cd14, Lgals3 and Tnfrsf12a-and decreased expression of anti-inflammatory markers such as Cd9 and Cd81

nodes.claim_text: In the kidney, the relative abundances of mesangial cells, capillary endothelial cells, loop of Henle ascending limb epithelial cells, and loop of Henle thick ascending limb epithelial cells all decreased with age.
nodes.experiment_detail: Droplet-based single-cell RNA-seq on kidney cells from C57BL/6JN mice at 1, 3, 18, 21, 24, and 30 months. n = 2,488, 2,832, 3,806, 2,257, 6,384 and 5,713 cells respectively.
nodes.analysis_type: cell type proportion analysis across age
nodes.analysis_detail: For each age group, the relative proportion of each cell type in the kidney was computed. Linear regression of relative cell type proportion against age was performed. Significance was assessed at P < 0.05 with r2 > 0.5 using two-sided Wald test with t-distribution.
nodes.evidence_location: Figure 2g; Main text - kidney composition (page 591-592); Supplementary Table 5
nodes.supporting_quote: Age-related changes in the kidney include a decrease in the relative abundance of mesangial cells, capillary endothelial cells, loop of Henle ascending limb epithelial cells and loop of Henle thick ascending limb epithelial cells

nodes.claim_text: Tissue-wide expression of Egf and Atp1a1 decreased with age in the kidney.
nodes.experiment_detail: Droplet-based single-cell RNA-seq on kidney cells from C57BL/6JN mice at 1, 3, 18, 21, 24, and 30 months.
nodes.analysis_type: differential gene expression analysis with age as continuous covariate
nodes.analysis_detail: Differential gene expression was performed on kidney tissue using MAST, treating age as a continuous numerical covariate while controlling for sex and technology. FDR < 0.01 and age coefficient > 0.005.
nodes.evidence_location: Figure 2h; Main text - kidney composition (page 591-592); Supplementary Table 6
nodes.supporting_quote: together with a tissue-wide reduction of Egf and Atp1a1 expression (Fig. 2h, Supplementary Table 6)-suggest an impaired glomerular filtration rate

nodes.claim_text: Local Atp1a1 expression increased with age in kidney capillary endothelial cells and mesangial cells despite the declining proportion of these cell types.
nodes.experiment_detail: Droplet-based single-cell RNA-seq on kidney cells from C57BL/6JN mice at 1, 3, 18, 21, 24, and 30 months.
nodes.analysis_type: cell-type-specific differential gene expression combined with proportion analysis
nodes.analysis_detail: Differential gene expression was performed within kidney capillary endothelial cells and mesangial cells separately using MAST, treating age as continuous covariate. The increase in local Atp1a1 expression was contrasted with the declining proportion of these cell types.
nodes.evidence_location: Main text - kidney composition (page 591-592); Supplementary Table 6
nodes.supporting_quote: Notably, local Atp1a1 expression increases with age in both capillary endothelial cells and mesangial cells, suggesting that a compensation mechanism counteracts the effects of the declining proportion of these cells with age.

nodes.claim_text: Tissue-wide expression of Umod decreased with age in the kidney, while Umod expression increased within the thick ascending limb epithelial cells that became less abundant.
nodes.experiment_detail: Droplet-based single-cell RNA-seq on kidney cells from C57BL/6JN mice at 1, 3, 18, 21, 24, and 30 months.
nodes.analysis_type: tissue-wide and cell-type-specific differential gene expression combined with proportion analysis
nodes.analysis_detail: Tissue-wide differential gene expression was performed using MAST with age as continuous covariate. Cell-type-specific analysis was performed within thick ascending limb epithelial cells. The increase in local Umod expression was contrasted with the declining proportion of these cells and the tissue-wide decrease.
nodes.evidence_location: Figure 2h; Main text - kidney composition (page 592); Supplementary Table 6
nodes.supporting_quote: the expression of Umod-which encodes uromodulin, the most abundant protein in urine-is also reduced across the tissue. The protein uromodulin is produced by the epithelial cells that line the thick ascending limb, and therefore-given the relative decrease in the proportion of epithelial cells in the ascending and the thick ascending limb-our results suggest that normal kidney functions are impaired

nodes.claim_text: In the spleen, the proportion of T cells decreased with age while the relative amount of plasma cells increased.
nodes.experiment_detail: Droplet-based single-cell RNA-seq on spleen cells from C57BL/6JN mice at 1, 3, 18, 21, 24, and 30 months. n = 2,986, 8,839, 7,141, 6,395, 5,245 and 8,946 cells respectively.
nodes.analysis_type: cell type proportion analysis across age
nodes.analysis_detail: For each age group, the relative proportion of T cells and plasma cells in the spleen was computed. Linear regression of relative cell type proportion against age was performed. Significance was assessed at P < 0.05 with r2 > 0.5 using two-sided Wald test with t-distribution.
nodes.evidence_location: Figure 2i; Main text - spleen composition (page 592); Supplementary Table 5
nodes.supporting_quote: In the spleen, the proportion of T cells decreases with age while the relative amount of plasma cells increases (Fig. 2i).

nodes.claim_text: Spleen differential gene expression showed upregulation of B cell and plasma cell markers (Cd79a, Jchain) and downregulation of the T cell marker Cd3d with age.

nodes.claim_text: An age-related increase in the number of somatic mutations was observed across all analysed organs using full-length transcript data from FACS.
nodes.experiment_detail: Full-length transcript single-cell RNA-seq data from FACS Smart-seq2 across all analysed organs at 3, 18, and 24 months. GATK HaplotypeCaller was used to identify single-nucleotide polymorphisms.
nodes.analysis_type: somatic mutation calling from transcriptomic data
nodes.analysis_detail: GATK HaplotypeCaller was used to call mutations from aligned FACS reads. Genes mutated in >60% of cells were removed to eliminate germline mutations. For each tissue, genes expressed in at least 75% of cells at all time points were selected. The average number of mutations per gene per cell was computed for each age group. The pairwise difference of mean mutations between age groups was computed.
nodes.evidence_location: Figure 3; Extended Data Figure 7; Main text - genomic instability / mutations (page 593)
nodes.supporting_quote: We focused on genes that were expressed in at least 75% of cells for each age group within a particular tissue, and observed an age-related increase in the number of mutations across all of the organs we analysed (Fig. 3, Extended Data Fig. 7a, c, e)

nodes.claim_text: Tongue and bladder were the most affected organs in terms of age-related accumulation of somatic mutations.

nodes.analysis_type: somatic mutation calling from transcriptomic data, cross-organ comparison
nodes.analysis_detail: After computing mean mutations per gene per cell for each tissue and age group, the tissues were ranked by the magnitude of age-related mutation increase. Tongue and bladder showed the largest increases.
nodes.evidence_location: Main text - genomic instability / mutations (page 593); Extended Data Figure 7
nodes.supporting_quote: tongue and bladder were the most affected.

nodes.claim_text: The observed age-related increase in mutations exceeded the level expected from amplification and sequencing errors as estimated using ERCC spike-in controls.

nodes.analysis_type: comparison of mutation rates in genes vs ERCC spike-in controls

nodes.evidence_location: Figure 3; Extended Data Figures 7-8; Main text - genomic instability / mutations (page 593)
nodes.supporting_quote: we verified that the number of mutations exceeded those expected from amplification and sequencing errors, which can be estimated using External RNA Controls Consortium (ERCC) controls that were spiked into each well

nodes.claim_text: Transcriptomic diversity analysis identified brain myeloid microglial cells and kidney macrophages as cell types with significant age-related changes in diversity.
nodes.experiment_type: single-cell RNA-seq (FACS Smart-seq2 and 10x Genomics droplet)
nodes.experiment_detail: Single-cell RNA-seq data from FACS (brain myeloid) and droplet (kidney) platforms across all age groups.
nodes.analysis_type: diversity score analysis across cell types and ages
nodes.analysis_detail: For each tissue-cell type-mouse combination, cells were clustered using 6 configurations (3 resolution parameters x 2 clustering methods: Louvain and Leiden). The Shannon entropy of cluster assignments was computed as the diversity score. Diversity scores were regressed against age using linear regression. FDR correction was applied for multiple comparisons. A tissue-cell type was selected if the slope was consistent across all 6 configurations and at least 2 of 6 had FDR < 0.3.
nodes.evidence_location: Figure 4c; Extended Data Figure 10; Extended Data Figure 11a-b; Main text - diversity score (page 593-594)
nodes.supporting_quote: We observed significant changes in diversity for cells of the immune system that originate from the brain and the kidney (Fig. 4c, Extended Data Fig. 11a, b).

nodes.claim_text: The age-related diversity changes in brain microglia and kidney macrophages were not confounded by the number of genes expressed per cell.
nodes.experiment_type: single-cell RNA-seq (FACS Smart-seq2 and 10x Genomics droplet)
nodes.experiment_detail: Single-cell RNA-seq data from FACS (brain myeloid) and droplet (kidney) platforms across all age groups.
nodes.analysis_type: confound analysis for diversity score
nodes.analysis_detail: The diversity score was correlated with the mean number of genes expressed per cell across all tissue-cell type combinations. Linear regression R2 values were computed for each clustering configuration to assess whether gene detection rate confounded the diversity score results.
nodes.evidence_location: Extended Data Figure 11c-d; Main text - diversity score (page 594)
nodes.supporting_quote: These results were not confounded by the number of genes expressed per cell (Extended Data Fig. 11c, d).

nodes.claim_text: In brain myeloid microglial cells, young (3-month) microglia predominantly occupied distinct transcriptomic clusters from old (18-month and 24-month) microglia.

nodes.claim_text: Old microglial clusters were enriched for interferon-responsive or regulatory genes including Oasl2, Oas1a, Ifit3, Rtp4, Bst2, Stat1, Irf7, Ifitm3, Usp18, Ifi204, and Ifit2.

nodes.claim_text: Approximately 55 of the top 200 differentially expressed genes between young and old microglial clusters overlapped with a previously reported Alzheimer's disease-specific microglial signature.

nodes.supporting_quote: the list of differentially expressed genes between 'young' and 'old' clusters resembles the previously reported Alzheimer's disease-specific microglial signature, with 55 out of the top 200 differential expressed genes shared between the two differential gene expression lists

nodes.claim_text: In kidney macrophages, a cluster predominantly composed of young (1-month and 3-month) cells was enriched for an M2-macrophage gene signature, while a cluster predominantly composed of old (18-month and older) cells resembled an M1-proinflammatory macrophage state.

nodes.claim_text: Changes in gene expression observed in bulk experiments are often attributable to both changes in the number of cells of a given type and changes in per-cell gene expression levels.
nodes.experiment_type: single-cell RNA-seq (FACS Smart-seq2 and 10x Genomics droplet)
nodes.experiment_detail: Single-cell RNA-seq data from FACS and droplet platforms were compared with bulk RNA-seq data from tissues (Schaum et al. 2019). Only male mice were used for comparison.
nodes.analysis_type: comparison of bulk and single-cell age-related gene expression changes
nodes.analysis_detail: For each tissue, single-cell data from male mice was analyzed. Cells were split into young (age <= 3 months) and old (age > 3 months) groups. For each gene, log2 fold change of cell count (fraction of cells expressing the gene) and read count (mean expression in expressing cells) between old and young was computed. Bulk RNA-seq gene expression was correlated with age using Spearman correlation. Genes were classified as increasing (rho > 0.7) or decreasing (rho < -0.7) with age in bulk data. Wilcoxon-Mann-Whitney test was used to assess whether single-cell log2 fold changes distinguished between bulk-defined up and down groups.
nodes.evidence_location: Extended Data Figure 5a-b; Main text - paragraph 3 (page 590)
nodes.supporting_quote: we observed that-in many cases-changes in gene expression are due to both changes in the numbers of cells in a population and changes in the gene expression levels in each cell

nodes.claim_text: B cells and endothelial cells independently annotated in different organs clustered together by unbiased whole-transcriptome analysis irrespective of tissue of origin or processing method.
nodes.experiment_type: single-cell RNA-seq (FACS Smart-seq2 and 10x Genomics droplet)
nodes.experiment_detail: All 356,213 cells from both FACS and droplet platforms were combined after batch correction using bbknn.
nodes.analysis_type: cross-tissue and cross-method clustering validation
nodes.analysis_detail: After bbknn batch correction, all cells were clustered using Louvain graph-based clustering. The co-occurrence of cells with the same annotation (e.g., B cells, endothelial cells) in the same clusters was assessed irrespective of tissue of origin or sequencing method.
nodes.evidence_location: Figure 1e-f; Extended Data Figure 1g-l
nodes.supporting_quote: cells annotated as B cells or endothelial cells tend to occupy the same clusters irrespective of their tissue of origin or the method with which they were processed

nodes.claim_text: More than 70% of cells in the dataset were automatically annotated using the Tabula Muris 3-month reference, with the remainder annotated manually by tissue experts.
nodes.experiment_type: single-cell RNA-seq (FACS Smart-seq2 and 10x Genomics droplet)
nodes.experiment_detail: Cell type annotation was performed on all cells across all age groups using the Tabula Muris 3-month data as reference.
nodes.analysis_type: semi-automated cell type annotation
nodes.analysis_detail: Each organ was analyzed independently across all ages. PCA was performed on most variable genes, followed by Louvain and Leiden clustering. The 3-month Tabula Muris data was subset and the mapping of cell types to clusters was assessed. Clusters with 1:1 mapping had annotations propagated to all ages. Clusters with 1:many mapping were flagged for manual validation. All automated annotations were reviewed by tissue experts.
nodes.evidence_location: Figure 1b; Main text - paragraph 2 (page 590)
nodes.supporting_quote: Using this approach, we were able to automatically annotate more than 70% of the cells. All the automated cell annotations were reviewed and approved by human experts, and the remaining cells were annotated by hand

nodes.claim_text: Senescence-associated genes E2f2, Lmnb1, Tnf, and Itgax showed positive correlation between ageing and the fraction of cells expressing them.
nodes.experiment_type: single-cell RNA-seq (FACS Smart-seq2 and 10x Genomics droplet)
nodes.experiment_detail: Single-cell RNA-seq data from all age groups across all tissues.
nodes.analysis_type: correlation of senescence marker expression fraction with age
nodes.analysis_detail: For each gene in a curated list of senescence markers (Supplementary Table 4), the fraction of cells expressing the gene was computed per age group across all tissues. The correlation between age and fraction of expressing cells was computed. E2f2, Lmnb1, Tnf, and Itgax showed positive correlations.
nodes.evidence_location: Main text - paragraph 3 (page 591); Supplementary Table 4
nodes.supporting_quote: other genes with positive correlation include E2f2, Lmnb1, Tnf and Itgax.

literature_candidates.claim_text: Ageing is characterized by a progressive loss of physiological integrity, leading to impaired function and increased vulnerability to death (Lopez-Otin et al. 2013).
literature_candidates.source_sentence: Ageing is characterized by a progressive loss of physiological integrity, leading to impaired function and increased vulnerability to death.
literature_candidates.reason: Scene-setting: this is a general definition of ageing that frames the paper but does not serve as a specific premise that any particular claim in the DAG structurally depends on.

literature_candidates.claim_text: Cdkn2a and its protein product p16 is one of the most frequently used markers of senescence and is an important hallmark of ageing (Rayess et al. 2012; Hernandez-Segura et al. 2018).
literature_candidates.source_sentence: The expression of Cdkn2a and its protein product p16 is one of the most frequently used markers of senescence and is an important hallmark of ageing.

literature_candidates.claim_text: Sirtuins are essential in delaying cellular senescence (Saunders & Verdin 2007; Lee et al. 2019).
literature_candidates.source_sentence: this is consistent with previous literature finding that sirtuins-the protein products of Sirt genes-are essential in delaying cellular senescence

literature_candidates.claim_text: Age-related urothelial changes are known to occur in the bladder (Daly et al. 2014).
literature_candidates.source_sentence: The observation that the proportion of bladder urothelial cells increases with age is concordant with known age-related urothelial changes.
literature_candidates.reason: Corroborative: the paper's own data independently establishes the increase in urothelial cells. The literature reference merely confirms agreement and does not provide an interpretation or premise that the paper's argument depends on.

literature_candidates.claim_text: Bladder ageing is associated with lower organ vascularization (Burmeister et al. 2013; Andersson et al. 2017).
literature_candidates.source_sentence: The decline of the endothelial population suggests that bladder ageing in mice might be associated with lower organ vascularization, which is consistent with recent findings
literature_candidates.reason: Corroborative: the paper's own data shows the endothelial decline and gene downregulation. The literature reference confirms agreement but does not provide a structurally necessary premise.

literature_candidates.claim_text: Overactive bladders are associated with inflammatory tissue microenvironments (Suskind 2017).
literature_candidates.source_sentence: The increase in the leukocyte population could indicate an inflammatory tissue microenvironment, a common hallmark of ageing that is consistent with literature on overactive bladders
literature_candidates.reason: Corroborative: the paper's own data shows the leukocyte increase and inflammatory gene expression. The literature reference provides context but is not structurally necessary.

literature_candidates.claim_text: Downregulation of ATP1A1 is associated with impaired function in renal cell carcinoma, and EGF serves as a prognostic biomarker in chronic kidney diseases (Zhang et al. 2017; Isaka 2016).
literature_candidates.source_sentence: together with a tissue-wide reduction of Egf and Atp1a1 expression (Fig. 2h, Supplementary Table 6)-suggest an impaired glomerular filtration rate

literature_candidates.claim_text: Uromodulin is the most abundant protein in urine and is produced by epithelial cells lining the thick ascending limb (Devuyst et al. 2017; Tokonami et al. 2018).
literature_candidates.source_sentence: the expression of Umod-which encodes uromodulin, the most abundant protein in urine-is also reduced across the tissue.
literature_candidates.reason: General textbook-level knowledge about uromodulin's identity and production site. The paper's own data shows the Umod expression changes; the literature provides basic gene annotation rather than resolving an ambiguous interpretation.

literature_candidates.claim_text: Age-related decline of T cell populations has been associated with increased risk of infectious disease and cancer (Palmer et al. 2018).
literature_candidates.source_sentence: Age-related decline of T cell populations has been associated with increased risk of infectious disease and cancer

literature_candidates.claim_text: Normal mammary gland involution is accompanied by increased expression of AP1 transcription factor family genes (Shen et al. 2006; Girnius et al. 2018).
literature_candidates.source_sentence: consistent with the observation that normal involution of the mammary gland is accompanied by increased expression of this gene family

literature_candidates.claim_text: Pro-inflammatory macrophages drive cellular senescence, and Il1b shows markedly different expression in the liver with age (Covarrubias et al. 2019).
literature_candidates.source_sentence: Previous findings suggested that pro-inflammatory macrophages drive cellular senescence, and identified the gene Il1b as showing markedly different expression in the liver with age

literature_candidates.claim_text: Specific blocking of IL1-RI in hepatocytes attenuates cell death after injury (Gehrke et al. 2018).
literature_candidates.source_sentence: Specific blocking of IL1-RI in hepatocytes has been shown to attenuate cell death after injury, supporting the idea that increased expression of Il1b in Kupffer cells is typically a poor prognostic

literature_candidates.claim_text: Liver sinusoidal endothelial cells are the main carriers of the mannose receptor (MRC1) in the liver (Liu et al. 2013).
literature_candidates.source_sentence: Liver sinusoidal endothelial cells (LSECs) have a unique role in immune defence, being the main carriers of the mannose receptor (MRC1) in this organ

literature_candidates.claim_text: LSECs have been found to have a reduced endocytic capacity in aged livers, and it has been suggested that LSECs proliferate after injury or that bone-marrow-derived LSEC progenitors are recruited to the liver.
literature_candidates.source_sentence: LSECs have been found to have a reduced endocytic capacity in aged livers, and it has been suggested that LSECs proliferate after injury or that bone-marrow-derived LSEC progenitors are recruited to the liver.
literature_candidates.reason: Corroborative/speculative context: the paper's own data shows the increase in Mrc1-expressing LSECs. This literature finding provides additional context but the paper does not structurally depend on it for any specific claim.

literature_candidates.claim_text: Genomic instability is among the most widely studied hallmarks of ageing (Lopez-Otin et al. 2013; Vijg & Suh 2013).
literature_candidates.source_sentence: Genomic instability is among the most widely studied hallmarks of ageing, and full-length transcript data enables analysis of the accumulation of somatic mutations with age.
literature_candidates.reason: Scene-setting: this is a general statement about the importance of genomic instability as a hallmark. The paper's mutation analysis stands on its own data without requiring this as a structural premise.

literature_candidates.claim_text: A unique microglia type associated with restricting development of Alzheimer's disease has been identified with a specific transcriptomic signature (Keren-Shaul et al. 2017).
literature_candidates.source_sentence: the list of differentially expressed genes between 'young' and 'old' clusters resembles the previously reported Alzheimer's disease-specific microglial signature

literature_candidates.claim_text: Older individuals have higher vulnerability to new infections and lower benefits from vaccination compared with younger individuals (Goronzy & Weyand 2013, 2017).
literature_candidates.source_sentence: This corroborates literature reports suggesting that older individuals have a higher vulnerability to new infections and lower benefits from vaccination compared with younger individuals

literature_candidates.claim_text: M1 and M2 macrophage states are defined by distinct marker gene sets (Jablonski et al. 2015).
literature_candidates.source_sentence: cluster 13 resembles a M1-proinflammatory macrophage state

literature_candidates.claim_text: Senescence markers have been previously characterized in multiple studies (Covarrubias et al. 2019; Nagano et al. 2016; Carnero 2013; Wang & Dreesen 2018).
literature_candidates.source_sentence: Using a list of previously characterized senescence markers, we plotted the fraction of cells expressing each marker across all age groups (Supplementary Table 4).
literature_candidates.reason: Citation pile: multiple references establishing that senescence markers exist. The specific marker list is a methodological input, not a structural premise the argument depends on.

literature_candidates.claim_text: Lmnb1 fluctuations have differential effects on cellular proliferation and senescence (Dreesen et al. 2013; Shah et al. 2013).
literature_candidates.source_sentence: other genes with positive correlation include E2f2, Lmnb1, Tnf and Itgax
literature_candidates.reason: Corroborative: the paper's own data shows the positive correlation of Lmnb1 with age. The literature provides background on Lmnb1's role but the paper does not use it to interpret an otherwise ambiguous result.

literature_candidates.claim_text: E2f2 regulation is linked to senescence through PML nuclear bodies (Vernier et al. 2011).
literature_candidates.source_sentence: other genes with positive correlation include E2f2, Lmnb1, Tnf and Itgax
literature_candidates.reason: Corroborative: the paper's own data shows the positive correlation. The literature reference provides mechanistic context but is not structurally required.

literature_candidates.claim_text: TNF-alpha promotes premature senescence via the PI3K/Akt signaling pathway (Li et al. 2017).
literature_candidates.source_sentence: other genes with positive correlation include E2f2, Lmnb1, Tnf and Itgax
literature_candidates.reason: Corroborative: the paper's own data shows the positive correlation of Tnf with age. The literature provides mechanistic context but is not structurally required.

literature_candidates.claim_text: Hammond et al. (2019) reported complex cell-state changes in microglia throughout the mouse lifespan.
literature_candidates.source_sentence: suggesting an expansion of this small pro-inflammatory subset of microglia in the ageing brain
literature_candidates.reason: Corroborative: the paper's own data independently establishes the microglial transcriptomic changes. The Hammond et al. reference is cited but not used as a structural premise.

literature_candidates.claim_text: The hallmarks of ageing include senescence, genomic instability, and changes in the immune system (Lopez-Otin et al. 2013).
literature_candidates.source_sentence: Using single-cell transcriptomic data, we assessed cell-type-specific manifestations of different hallmarks of ageing-such as senescence, genomic instability and changes in the immune system.
literature_candidates.reason: Scene-setting: this is a general framework from Lopez-Otin et al. that motivates the paper's organization but the paper's specific claims about each hallmark stand on their own data. The framework is too general to serve as a specific structural premise.
